Notes to Editors
World Chess (LSE: CHSS) is a London-based chess gaming and entertainment company and Fédération Internationale des Échecs ("FIDE") official commercial partner. World Chess organised the FIDE Championship Matches in the USA and the UK and revolutionised the sport by signing the biggest media partnerships in chess history. World Chess develops Armageddon, the chess league for prime-time television, and operates worldchess.com, the official FIDE-rated gaming platform.
More at worldchess.com.

latest.title: Subscription Agreement
latest.content:
Today 09:10
RNS Number : 5361U
World Chess PLC
26 February 2026
26 February 2026
World Chess Plc
("World Chess" or the "Company")
Subscription Agreement
World Chess Plc (LSE: CHSS), the London-listed chess company and official commercial partner of FIDE, is pleased to announce that it has entered a binding subscription arrangement with two of the Company's existing shareholders (together, the "Investors"), raising approximately £1,154,941 (the "Subscription").
Under the terms of the respective agreements, the Company will issue a total of 175,915,198 new ordinary shares of £0.0001 each (the "Subscription Shares") at an issue price of £0.00656533 per share (the "Issue Price"). The Subscription Shares will be allotted and issued fully paid and will rank pari passu with the Company's existing ordinary shares in all respects.
Completion of the Subscription is subject to the shareholder authority to issue shares to be proposed at a general meeting of the Company being held on 18 March 2026. Should such authority not be obtained, the number of shares to be issued to the Investors will be reduced to 127,605,998, being the current remaining share issuance authority.
As part of this transaction, the period for the fulfilment of the conditions of the warrants referred to in the Company's announcement dated 23 July 2025 will be extended by two years, from 31 December 2026 to 31 December 2028.
Ilya Merenzon, Chief Executive Officer of World Chess Plc, commented:
"This new capital allows us to fund our next phase of growth, and the continued commitment of our existing shareholders gives us both the resources and the confidence to deliver on it."
This announcement contains information which, prior to its disclosure, was inside information as stipulated under Regulation 11 of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019/310 (as amended).
For more information, visit: www.worldchess.com or contact:
World Chess Plc
Ilya Merenzon, CEO

AlbR Capital Limited

latest.title: Board Changes
latest.content:
12 Feb 2026 13:52
RNS Number : 8217S
World Chess PLC
12 February 2026
World Chess Plc
("World Chess" or the "Company" or the "Group")
Board Changes
World Chess (LSE: CHSS), a leading chess organisation that develops and operates worldchess.com, the official online gaming platform of the International Chess Federation (FIDE),
announces that Neil Rafferty has resigned from the Board of Directors, effective from 11 May 2026.
Mr Rafferty has given three months' notice in accordance with his contractual obligations and will continue to fulfil his duties as Interim Chair and Director until 11 May 2026, or until a new Chairman is appointed, whichever occurs first.
Statement by the Board
The Board would like to express its sincere gratitude to Neil for his valuable contribution and dedicated service to World Chess since the Company's IPO. Neil has played an important role during a significant period in the Company's development, and his insights and commitment have been greatly appreciated.The Board thanks Neil for his service and wish him every success in his future endeavours.
Chairman Succession
The Company has commenced a formal process to appoint a new Chair at the earliest opportunity.
This announcement contains information which, prior to its disclosure, was inside information as stipulated under Regulation 11 of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019/310 (as amended).
For more information, visit:
www.worldchess.com
or contact:
World Chess
Ilya Merenzon, CEO
merenzon@worldchess.com
AlbR Capital Limited - Financial Adviser
David Coffman / Dan Harris
+44 (0) 20 7469 0930
Notes to Editors
About World Chess Plc
World Chess (LSE: CHSS) is a leading chess organisation that develops and operates worldchess.com, a subscription platform with over one million users and the official online platform of the International Chess Federation (FIDE). The Company organized several World Chess Championships, including in New York and London. It organises the Armageddon Series and produces The World Chess Show, distributed across more than 50 international markets. More at worldchess.com.

latest.title: Total Voting Rights
latest.content:
Today 18:14
RNS Number : 1866R
World Chess PLC
30 January 2026
World Chess Plc
("World Chess" or the "Company")
Total Voting Rights
In accordance with the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules, as at 30 January 2026, the Company's issued share capital consists of
887,938,480
Ordinary Shares of £0.0001, each with one voting right. The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in treasury. Therefore, the total number of voting rights in the Company is
887,938,480
.
The above figure of 887,938,480
should be used by shareholders in the Company as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the share capital of the Company under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
For more information, visit:

or contact:

company_30s.what_they_do: World Chess Plc runs a global network that monetises chess through streaming, live events, sponsorships and a proprietary digital platform that hosts tournaments, lessons and community forums.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Chess has a worldwide fan base that is growing thanks to e‑sports, mobile apps and corporate sponsorships. The company owns the World Chess Show, a flagship TV and online program, and has partnerships that place it at the heart of the sport’s commercial ecosystem.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is in a cash‑burn phase, raising capital via a subscription agreement and making board changes to shore up governance. It is still pre‑profit, with revenue largely coming from sponsorship and content licensing. The share price sits at the lower end of its 52‑week range, reflecting a deep slide from its all‑time high.

rns_narrative: The company is restructuring its board and launching a new subscription round to raise fresh capital. The latest RNS shows a mix of governance changes, a partnership with Algorand Foundation to create a blockchain‑based token, and an expansion of its flagship show into new markets.
These moves suggest the firm is actively seeking liquidity and a new revenue stream, while also trying to stabilize management after a series of director resignations.
The narrative now revolves around whether the subscription can close and whether the new partnerships will generate sustainable cash flow.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Mixed uncertainty – some fans see a strategic pivot, others fear a cash‑drained collapse.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls point to the subscription round and the Algorand partnership as potential catalysts that could inject capital and create a new token economy.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears highlight the company’s lack of profitability, the resignations of key directors, and the risk that the subscription will fail to attract investors.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has shifted from cautious optimism in early 2026 to a more skeptical stance after the board changes and the announcement that the subscription may only cover a few months of burn.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – no public chatter, so any news will create a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: Closing of the subscription agreement in the next 30–45 days – a successful raise would immediately lift the share price and signal market confidence.
Announcement of the new blockchain token tied to the Algorand partnership, which could open a new revenue channel and attract a niche investor base.
Results of the upcoming quarterly earnings (if any) that show improved cash flow or a clear path to profitability.
A volume spike that exceeds 2× the average daily trade, indicating fresh institutional interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sharp uptick in daily volume coupled with a breakout above the recent low. Also monitor any further board appointments or changes in the subscription status – a delay or cancellation would be a red flag.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stage of a deep crash recovery. If the subscription closes, the next step is a short‑term bounce from the bottom, followed by a consolidation at a new support level before a longer‑term rebound.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: A deep crash of 70‑95% sets the stage for a large mean‑reversion move. If the subscription raises enough capital, the price could recover to roughly half its former peak, implying a 35‑40% upside from the current level. Historical deep‑crash signals have delivered 39‑162% returns, so the potential reward is substantial.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company still has no profit, a high burn rate, and a fragile governance structure. If the subscription fails or the token launch stalls, the share could slide further, potentially wiping out the remaining upside. The worst‑case is a continued decline to the bottom of the 52‑week range or a forced liquidation.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is limited to the current price (the bottom of the crash). Potential gain is 35‑40% above that level, giving a risk‑reward ratio of roughly 1:1.5 to 1:2 when factoring in the probability of a successful subscription.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter once the subscription is confirmed closed – place a limit order a few pips below the breakout level to capture the initial move. Size the position so that a 10‑15% drop would wipe out 2‑3% of your account, keeping risk in check. Set a stop just below the recent low to protect against a failed launch.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see a volume spike that doubles the 10‑day average and a clear announcement of subscription closure. If the price stays flat for two weeks after the announcement, consider tightening the stop or exiting.
playbook.if_passing: If the company announces a winding‑up, bankruptcy filing, or a delisting notice, exit immediately. No other terminal risk is currently evident.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A 70‑plus‑percent crash, a looming subscription round and board overhaul create a high‑reward, low‑downside play that hinges on a successful capital raise – a classic contrarian set‑up.
